channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Legal Developments in Lissu's Case Highlight Political Tensions in Tanzania
item.description: Tundu Lissu, a prominent Tanzanian opposition figure, faces ongoing legal challenges as his lawyer's emotional display in court has drawn public attention. The case, which involves new evidence, under...

item.title: China accuses Philippines of violating 2024 agreement during Ayungin incident
item.description: Chinese Ambassador Jing Quan accused the Philippines of breaching a 2024 provisional agreement during a tense standoff at Ayungin Shoal, where Philippine Navy personnel allegedly conducted unauthorize...

item.title: NEET protest and political tensions as leaders detained and released
item.description: Thousands of protesters returned to Jantar Mantar to continue their agitation following the release of Congress leaders Rahul Gandhi and Priyanka Gandhi Vadra after being detained by Delhi Police duri...

item.title: Deputy PM Ishaq Dar attends Asean Regional Forum ministerial meeting in Manila
item.description: Deputy Prime Minister and Foreign Minister Ishaq Dar arrived in Manila on Wednesday for the 33rd Asean Regional Forum (ARF) ministerial meeting, as reported by Pakistan's Foreign Office. The event bri...

item.title: Over 2,200 Dangerous Wild Animals Kept as Pets in Britain, Study Reveals
item.description: A study by Born Free has found that over 2,200 dangerous wild animals, including venomous snakes, big cats, and crocodiles, are legally kept as pets in Britain under the 50-year-old Dangerous Wild Ani...

item.title: Rescued Eagle Jackie in Critical Condition After Anemia and Inflammation
item.description: An eagle believed to be nest cam star Jackie, rescued from Big Bear Lake, is battling anemia and inflamed kidneys at the Ojai Raptor Center. Friends of Big Bear Valley confirmed her identity based on ...

item.title: Defense Secretary Pegs U.S. War Against Iran at $37.5 Billion Cost
item.description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth testified before the Senate Committee on the cost of the U.S. war against Iran, estimating the total expenditure at approximately $37.5 billion. The hearing highlighted ...

item.title: US and Saudi Arabia to Finalize Civil Nuclear Cooperation Agreement
item.description: The United States is set to announce a significant nuclear cooperation agreement with Saudi Arabia, according to media reports. The deal, which will provide billions in funding for the US nuclear indu...

item.title: First Nations Leaders Call for Inquiry and Resignations Amid Ontario Wildfire Response Criticisms
item.description: First Nations leaders from Northern Ontario have demanded a public inquiry and the resignation of provincial officials following criticism of the government's wildfire response. At a news conference i...

item.title: Tigray's Ancient Grapevines: A Genetic Treasure Preserved Through Generations
item.description: New research has uncovered the genetic and historical significance of Tigray’s ancient grapevines, which have thrived despite challenges. These vines, grown in household compounds and village gardens,...

item.title: Australia Issues Travel Warnings for Middle East Amid Escalating Conflict
item.description: The Australian government has upgraded travel advisories for Oman, Jordan, and Saudi Arabia to level three, urging citizens to reconsider non-essential trips due to escalating conflict in the Middle E...

item.title: Canada Warns Citizens About Security Risks in Kenya
item.description: Canada has issued a formal travel advisory urging its citizens to exercise extreme caution when visiting Kenya due to persistent security threats. The advisory highlights risks such as terrorism, viol...

item.title: Mercedes-Benz SUV crashes into residential wall after losing control
item.description: A white Mercedes-Benz SUV crashed into the boundary wall of the Summerville residential complex, causing significant damage. The incident occurred on Monday afternoon when the vehicle lost control whi...

item.title: Conductor Allegedly Spread Remittances and Faked Arrest
item.description: The article reports on a incident involving a conductor who allegedly dispersed remittances and pretended to be detained. Christopher Sitson, ABS-CBN News, detailed the case where the individual claim...

item.title: Kamaraj's Governance Legacy: Lessons in Leadership and Development
item.description: The article explores the enduring impact of K. Kamaraj, former Chief Minister of Madras State, on modern Tamil Nadu governance. It highlights his people-centric approach, administrative acumen, and em...

item.title: Philippines and China maintain South China Sea code of conduct stance
item.description: The article discusses the ongoing diplomatic efforts between the Philippines and China regarding the South China Sea code of conduct. Despite tensions, both nations continue to emphasize the importanc...

item.title: Upcoming Football Competitions After the 2026 FIFA World Cup
item.description: The 2026 FIFA World Cup has concluded, but fans can still enjoy a packed schedule of club competitions and leagues across Europe, the US, and Australia. The A-League Men's competition in Australia wil...

item.title: Ibrahim Ambwere's Journey from Sweeper to Billionaire and His Teacher Payment Offer
item.description: The article details the life of Ibrahim Ambwere, a self-made billionaire from Western Kenya, and his daughter Melody Sinzore, a prominent radio presenter. Ambwere, who started as a sweeper and carpent...

item.title: Johannesburg and National Treasury Finalise Financial Stabilisation Measures for City
item.description: The City of Johannesburg and National Treasury have reached an agreement on immediate financial stabilisation measures to address the metro's severe liquidity crisis. With less than two weeks of cash ...

item.title: UK Inflation Expected to Drop in June Amid Oil Price Falls and Energy Cost Concerns
item.description: Official UK inflation data for June is expected to show a temporary decline, driven by falling petrol and diesel prices following an interim ceasefire between the US and Iran. Economists anticipate th...
